name: Pulumi Cloud API
what_it_does: Pulumi is an infrastructure-as-code platform where you write real programming languages (Python, TypeScript, Go, Java, C#) instead of YAML/HCL to define cloud infrastructure. Pulumi Cloud provides state management, team collaboration, CI/CD integration, secrets management, and policy enforcement. The REST API exposes stack management, deployment triggers, update history, and resource state — enabling agents to provision, update, and destroy infrastructure programmatically.
use_cases: Triggering infrastructure deployments from CI/CD pipelines via API
Querying current infrastructure state and resource inventory
Managing stack configuration and secrets programmatically
Building platform engineering portals that provision infrastructure on demand
Rollback and state management for failed deployments
Multi-cloud infrastructure management from a single API surface
not_for: Teams deeply invested in Terraform/OpenTofu (migration cost is high)
Simple static infrastructure that doesn't benefit from general-purpose programming languages
Environments where Pulumi CLI cannot be installed (use Terraform Cloud API for that)
best_when: Your team writes Python or TypeScript and wants infrastructure defined in the same language as application code, with the ability to use loops, functions, and libraries in IaC definitions. Especially good for complex dynamic infrastructure that is awkward in Terraform HCL.
avoid_when: Your team is already proficient in Terraform, your organization has standardized on HCL, or you need a larger provider ecosystem (Terraform has more providers).

alternatives.reason: Terraform Cloud is the dominant IaC platform with larger provider ecosystem; Pulumi is better for teams preferring general-purpose languages over HCL
GitHub Actions can trigger deployments; Pulumi Cloud API provides more granular infrastructure state access

auth.notes: Pulumi Cloud API uses personal or organization access tokens (PULUMI_ACCESS_TOKEN). Passed as Authorization: token {TOKEN}. Tokens scoped to organization or personal. No OAuth for machine-to-machine auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Individual: free forever with up to 150,000 state updates/year and 1 user. No credit card required.
pricing.paid_tiers: Team Starter: $50/month for 3 users. Team: $500/month for 10 users. Enterprise: custom. Per-user seat pricing for teams.
pricing.notes: Open source Pulumi engine is free. Pulumi Cloud is the managed backend — you can also self-host state in S3/GCS/Azure Blob for free. Paid features: RBAC, SSO, policy enforcement, audit logs.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not publicly documented. Deployments are queued and run asynchronously — polling required for completion status.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard HTTP codes with JSON error bodies. Deployment failures include structured error logs from the Pulumi engine. State conflicts return clear error messages with conflict details.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Infrastructure deployments are declarative and idempotent — running the same program twice results in no changes if state matches desired state. API calls for state reads are fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Deployments are async — agents must poll the update status endpoint or use webhooks; don't assume completion after triggering
Stack locks prevent concurrent deployments — agents that launch parallel deployments on the same stack will fail with lock errors
Resource graph ordering is computed by Pulumi engine; API cannot reorder resource creation
Secrets are encrypted at rest — agents cannot read secret values back through the API (write-only)
Program runtime (Python/TypeScript) runs locally or in Pulumi Deployments service, not in the API call itself
Stack outputs are available via API only after successful deployment — agents that query outputs before deploy completes get stale values
